title: Bulk Admin Controls for Board Structure

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Boards currently require most structural changes to be performed one element at a time. Managing groups, columns, and status or dropdown labels individually becomes time-consuming and error-prone on large boards, templates, or long-running enterprise workflows.

As boards evolve through duplication, migration, testing, and redesign, they often accumulate outdated groups, unused columns, and legacy status labels. Without bulk administrative controls, cleanup, standardization, and governance do not scale, making it difficult to maintain consistency across reused or long-lived boards.

What are you trying to achieve

Enable admins to manage board structure efficiently at scale by providing bulk administrative controls for groups, columns, and status or dropdown fields. This includes the ability to select and delete multiple groups or columns at once, sort and lock group order, and reset or remove status or dropdown labels in a single action, with appropriate safeguards.

These capabilities would significantly reduce manual effort, prevent errors during redesigns or migrations, support consistent board and template structures, and make enterprise-grade governance and long-term maintainability feasible across large accounts.
